name: SCM Master POC 4 — Capacity Over-Order Guard

nodes.name: Order + Capacity
nodes.notes: An order request (150 units) against a warehouse: cap 200, 26 on hand, 78 already inbound. Free-to-order = 200 - 26 - 78 = 96. So 150 must be CLAMPED to 96. Edit requested_units to 50 to see it pass.

nodes.name: Over-Order Guard (no AI)
nodes.notes: The guard is DETERMINISTIC and enforced here — no AI involved. An over-order is refused or clamped, never placed. This is the 'never over-order' invariant the MVP enforces server-side (HTTP 422).

nodes.name: PLACE the order
nodes.notes: TRUE branch: it fits → proceed to stage/place.

nodes.name: REFUSE / CLAMP (422)
nodes.notes: FALSE branch: clamp to free-to-order, or reject if full. In the MVP this is an HTTP 422 the buyer sees in the New-Order modal.
